definition: A complete submission to the CAA containing all regulatory evidence, safety cases, operational procedures, and technical documentation required for a BVLOS UAS trial to proceed
notes: Core term but 'complete' is undefined. Synonyms include 'submission' and 'application'. Unclear whether 'pack' and 'application' are synonymous.

definition: Specific Operational Risk Assessment - a regulatory framework for assessing UAS operational risks. Version 2.5 is referenced in requirements (JARUS SORA v2.5)
notes: Acronym expanded in context but not in the user stories themselves. REQ002 uses 'SORA evidence' without defining what qualifies.

definition: Risk assessment documentation demonstrating how a swarm of UAS operating collectively (rather than individually) presents different operational risks, with evidence specific to coordinated multi-aircraft behaviour
notes: Critical undefined term. 'Swarm-aware' is never defined. What constitutes 'evidence' for swarm behaviour is ambiguous. Unclear what distinguishes swarm-aware SORA from standard SORA.

definition: Specific Assurance and Integrity Level - a risk classification level determined by SORA assessment, used to set operational and design requirements
notes: Acronym not expanded in requirements. Assumed domain knowledge. Unclear how SAIL relates to OSO or SORA.

definition: Operational Safety Objectives - specific safety goals derived from SORA assessment that the operational design and procedures must satisfy
notes: Acronym not expanded. Used interchangeably with 'requirements' but distinction unclear.

definition: In a manner that can be justified and substantiated through evidence and logical argument to regulatory scrutiny
notes: Qualitative adjective. No criteria given for what makes a decision or setting defensible. High stakes term.

definition: Concept of Operations - a narrative description of how the trial will be conducted operationally, including flight profiles, crew roles, decision points, and normal procedures
notes: Acronym not expanded in user story. Standard aerospace term but context-specific interpretation needed.

definition: Operational Design Domain - the operating conditions (environmental, technical, geographical, temporal) for which the UAS and its autonomy systems have been validated and are approved to operate
notes: Acronym not expanded. Critical for autonomy assurance but definition left implicit.

definition: Expected, planned system performance under normal operating conditions as described in ConOps
notes: Paired with 'worst-credible' as a duality. Definition derivable but explicit threshold undefined.

definition: The most severe adverse system performance or failure mode that is reasonably foreseeable and must be survived by containment, backup systems, or crew intervention
notes: Ambiguous boundary: what is 'credible' vs 'incredible'? Who judges this? No quantitative threshold given.

definition: Temporary Danger Area - a designated airspace volume and time window reserved for the UAS trial, excluding other civilian traffic and within which certain rules apply
notes: Acronym not expanded. CAA-specific designation but constraints and activation procedures undefined.

definition: To separate the trial airspace and operations from other legitimate airspace users through procedural, technical, or physical means to prevent conflicts
notes: Verb used in regulatory context. Mechanisms (TDA, NOTAM, coordination) implied but not explicit.

definition: Technical and procedural systems that prevent a UAS from leaving its authorized operational area due to control loss, autonomy failure, or link loss
notes: Critical safety term. Distinction between 'automatic containment' and other forms undefined. No specification of tolerance, fail-safe assumptions, or design standards.

definition: Loss of control of a UAS such that it exits its authorized airspace and cannot be recovered by the operator
notes: Domain jargon. Used to define the failure mode that containment prevents.

definition: Beyond Visual Line of Sight - operating an aircraft without direct visual contact by the pilot, relying on instrumentation, cameras, or other remote monitoring
notes: Acronym expanded in context. Standard aviation term. Distinction from VLOS and EVLOS not covered in requirements.

definition: Command and Control link - the communication pathway between the ground crew and the UAS for transmitting commands and receiving status/telemetry data
notes: Acronym expanded in context. 'Authorised' and 'monitored' are added qualifiers but not defined.

definition: Command-and-control communications that have been approved by spectrum authorities and are continuously observed to confirm link health, integrity, and absence of interference
notes: 'Authorised' and 'monitored' both undefined. What triggers non-compliance? Who monitors? What are failure thresholds?

definition: Communication pathways between individual UAS aircraft within the swarm, used for coordination, de-confliction, or status exchange
notes: Assumes swarm architecture with aircraft-to-aircraft comms. Distinction from C2 (ground-to-air) unclear in context of link-loss survival.

definition: The UAS and swarm have procedures and design such that loss of either C2 or inter-vehicle communication can be managed without loss of separation, collision, or uncontrolled flight
notes: Ambiguous: what is 'survivable'? Defined by mission, location, or crew capability? No time-to-recover or failure-mode boundary specified.

definition: Electromagnetic emissions from the UAS communication systems do not cause harmful interference to other authorized spectrum users
notes: Technical regulatory term. No threshold, measurement method, or standard referenced. Assumes familiarity with spectrum regulations.

definition: RTCA DO-365 - a standard for performance of Detect-and-Avoid systems for unmanned aircraft systems
notes: Standard referenced but full scope, applicability, and mandatory vs. guiding status unclear in this context.

definition: ASTM F3442 - standard for design and performance of detect-and-avoid systems for small unmanned aircraft systems in beyond visual line of sight operations
notes: Acronym not expanded; assumed domain knowledge. Used alongside DO-365 but relationship and priority undefined.

definition: Detect-and-Avoid - a system that senses other aircraft and airspace hazards and automatically or advisorily initiates avoidance maneuvers to maintain safe separation
notes: Acronym expanded in context. Distinction between autonomous avoidance and crew-assisted avoidance not specified.

definition: A CAA subject matter expert responsible for evaluating Detect-and-Avoid system design, testing, and performance against applicable standards
notes: Role definition implied. Authority and decision criteria not specified.

definition: An airspace encounter between the UAS and another aircraft that is cooperative (e.g., within a segregated zone with known participants, or with aircraft equipped with transponders and ADS-B)
notes: Contrast to non-cooperative implies information asymmetry. Definition based on inference.

definition: An airspace encounter with another aircraft that has not coordinated with the UAS trial and may not be aware of the UAS presence or transmit position information
notes: Highest-risk scenario. Criteria for 'credible' non-cooperative encounters and test coverage undefined.

definition: Avoidance outcomes are consistent, safe, and align with regulatory requirements regardless of encounter geometry, aircraft performance differences, or weather conditions
notes: High-stakes qualifier. 'Predictably' is subjective. What are acceptable resolution strategies? Whose perspective (own, other aircraft, safety)?

definition: Multi-aircraft separation assurance logic in which the swarm members coordinate their avoidance maneuvers to resolve potential conflicts between each other and external aircraft
notes: Critical for swarm safety. Collision avoidance between swarm members vs. external aircraft may have different priorities. Undefined.

definition: Multiple UAS operating in coordinated flight, sharing a common mission objective, with decision-making authority distributed or centralized across the fleet
notes: Core concept but boundaries undefined. Minimum/maximum aircraft count? Coordination mechanism (wireless, pre-planned, real-time)? Distinct from 'formation' or 'multi-agent system'?

definition: The design of how operational and safety decisions are made within the swarm, including whether decisions are made autonomously, by distributed consensus, by a lead aircraft, or by ground crew
notes: Definition inferred. Scope includes autonomy, hierarchy, human authority, and failure modes.

definition: Predefined constraints on swarm behaviour, decision-making, or autonomy that are enforced or monitored by the onboard systems to prevent deviation from the Operational Design Domain
notes: Ambiguous. Are these hard constraints, soft limits, or advisory bounds? Who sets them?

definition: Collective behaviour of the swarm that arises from local interactions between aircraft and is not explicitly commanded, potentially including undesired or unexpected outcomes
notes: Computer science term. In safety context, implies behaviors not fully predictable from component specifications. Evidence of what?

definition: To provide objective evidence and argument that a system design and its implementation meet regulatory requirements and safety objectives
notes: Regulatory assurance verb. Distinct from 'assess' or 'evaluate'. No criterion for what constitutes sufficient assurance.

definition: Capable of being subjected to analysis, testing, and verification such that safety and regulatory objectives can be reliably demonstrated
notes: Critical qualifier but undefined. What makes an ML component more or less assurable?

definition: Assurance and Certification of Machine Learning for Aviation Systems - a framework for assurance of ML components used in aviation
notes: Acronym not expanded in user story. Framework status (mandatory vs. guidance) and scope undefined.

definition: Safety-critical autonomy certification and evaluation - a structured approach to demonstrating safety and assurance of autonomous systems
notes: Acronym not expanded. Assumed domain knowledge. Relationship to AMLAS unclear.

definition: A declaration and documentation of the specific version, training data, performance metrics, and operational constraints of each ML model deployed in the trial, such that the exact model can be reconstructed for post-incident analysis
notes: Highly specialized term. 'Frozen' implies immutable, but means, scope, and retention period undefined. 'Manifest' suggests a structured document but format not specified.

definition: A structured argument supported by evidence that machine learning components are designed, trained, deployed, and monitored in accordance with safety and regulatory requirements
notes: Derives from Goal Structuring Notation assurance cases. Scope relative to overall system assurance case undefined.

definition: Capable of detailed post-hoc analysis and explanation of why an ML model made a specific decision in a specific circumstance, sufficient for accident investigation and regulatory review
notes: Critical high-stakes term. 'Forensically' implies legal/investigative standard but scope and methods undefined. Distinct from real-time 'explainability'.

definition: Documentation and tools demonstrating that decisions made by ML models can be understood, justified, and traced to input data and model parameters
notes: Separate from 'forensic explainability'. Form and sufficiency of evidence undefined.

definition: Active, informed oversight by crew that retains the ability to perceive, understand, and intervene in swarm operations before safety-critical outcomes occur
notes: High-stakes, heavily debated term. No definition of 'meaningful', thresholds for authority, or failure modes when control is lost.

definition: The mental and physical effort required by crew to monitor and control the swarm, assessed against human factors principles to ensure fatigue, distraction, or saturation does not degrade safety
notes: Human factors term. Standards (RAMP, NASA-TLX, etc.) and acceptable thresholds not specified.

definition: The ability and time required for human crew to transition from monitoring to active control of the swarm in response to an off-nominal event
notes: Critical safety concept. Time-to-takeover, mode transition procedure, and success criteria undefined.

definition: Flight termination capability that operates independently of C2 link, crew commands, and onboard autonomy, triggered by onboard geofence or energy-state limits to ensure automatic safe ending of flight in any failure scenario
notes: Safety-critical system. Independence criteria and test methods undefined. Relationship to containment unclear.

definition: A table or structured document mapping off-nominal operational states (system failures, environmental hazards, loss of comms) to the crew procedures and automated responses that safely mitigate each failure
notes: Format and completeness criteria undefined. What constitutes 'complete' coverage of contingencies?

definition: Air Traffic Service Unit - the air traffic control facility or service provider responsible for the airspace in which the trial operates
notes: Acronym not expanded. Coordination role and authority boundaries undefined.

definition: Any operational condition that deviates from the planned ConOps, including system failures, environmental changes, loss of comms, or crew incapacitation
notes: Complement to 'nominal behaviour'. Enumeration of what constitutes off-nominal undefined.

definition: Free from unacceptable risk of harm to people, property, or the environment, meeting regulatory thresholds and industry standards for the specific operation
notes: Foundational safety term but no absolute definition. Risk acceptance criteria and decision authority undefined.

definition: Continuous data logging of all aircraft states, crew actions, autonomy decisions, and external events during the trial, with sufficient fidelity and retention to enable post-incident investigation
notes: Ambiguous: what constitutes 'complete'? Sampling rates, data types, retention period undefined. Contrast with 'frozen-model manifest' and 'explainability evidence'.

definition: Mandatory Occurrence Report - a CAA/UK aviation authority requirement to report specific incident categories for investigation and systemic learning
notes: Acronym not expanded. Reporting triggers and obligations to the regulator assumed known.

definition: Legal and regulatory duties to report safety incidents to the CAA that meet threshold criteria, with timing, content, and confidentiality requirements
notes: Specific to UK regulatory regime. Thresholds and procedures not detailed.

definition: Data Protection Impact Assessment - a systematic evaluation of privacy risks when processing personal data, particularly GDPR-relevant data
notes: Acronym not expanded. Scope of 'personal data' in trial context undefined (crew, observers, tracked aircraft, ground personnel).

definition: An occurrence during or after the trial that involves an aircraft accident, serious incident, or occurrence with safety significance requiring investigation
notes: Regulatory definition varies. Thresholds for 'serious' or 'significance' undefined.

definition: Capable of being thoroughly analyzed by qualified investigators using available data, procedures, and technical expertise to determine root causes and systemic factors
notes: Evidence quality and sufficiency for investigation undefined.

definition: Third-party liability insurance covering bodily injury, property damage, and environmental harm caused by the UAS trial to persons or assets on the ground
notes: Coverage limits, exclusions, and insurance company approval by CAA not specified.

definition: A documented assessment of the trial area identifying ground hazards, population density, critical infrastructure, and sensitive sites to justify risk acceptance and insurance adequacy
notes: Survey standards, required detail, and approval criteria undefined.

definition: The probability and consequence of harm to persons, property, or assets not directly involved in the trial (i.e., those on the ground outside the trial footprint)
notes: Risk definition implied. Acceptable thresholds and calculation method undefined.

definition: Capable of being shifted from the trial operator to the insurance company, such that financial consequences of incidents are covered by insurance rather than borne by the operator
notes: Insurance concept. Limits and exceptions to transferability not specified.

definition: Based on verified data and evidence rather than assumptions or theoretical models, such that risk assessments reflect observable conditions
notes: Qualitative criterion. 'Factually' implies verification standard but method undefined.

definition: Demonstrated knowledge, skills, and experience required to safely plan, execute, and supervise the UAS trial within the approved operational parameters
notes: Human factors and licensing concept. Specific competency domains (airmanship, swarm ops, autonomy, etc.) and assessment methods undefined.

definition: Documented proof of competency through training records, examinations, simulator performance, or flight experience that meets CAA licensing or authorization standards
notes: Evidence types and standards for BVLOS swarm operations undefined. Existing licenses adequate or new qualification needed?

definition: Structured preparation and instruction of pilots, observers, and safety personnel to operate the swarm within the ConOps, handle contingencies, and comply with regulatory obligations
notes: Training scope, duration, and proficiency assessment method undefined.

definition: A formal allocation of safety responsibilities, regulatory authorities, and decision-making jurisdictions between the CAA and other regulatory bodies (e.g., MOD, MAA) to ensure no gaps or conflicts
notes: Regulatory governance term. Specific allocation between CAA and MAA (Ministry of Aviation Authority or similar) undefined. Document form and approval process unknown.

definition: Without overlap or omission of regulatory authority or responsibility, such that every aspect of the trial has a designated regulator or authority
notes: Criterion for completeness undefined. What constitutes a 'gap'? How is it verified?

definition: A sequence of decision points in the trial build-up at which new operational phases (increased complexity, aircraft count, autonomy, geography) can only proceed upon independent review and approval of accumulated evidence
notes: Program management structure. Number, timing, and success criteria for each gate undefined. Who approves progression?

definition: An incremental approach to the trial in which operational envelope is expanded in planned steps, each with defined success criteria and approval gates
notes: Inverse risk management concept. Phase definitions and approval authority undefined.

definition: Safety and operational evidence accumulates through successive trial phases such that each phase generates data validating the expanded envelope of the next phase
notes: Evidence type, volume, and statistical sufficiency for progression undefined.

definition: Capable of returning to a previous trial phase or pausing if evidence from a new phase indicates unacceptable risk or performance deviation
notes: Operational governance concept. Triggers and procedures for reversal undefined.

definition: CAA Publication 722 - the UK regulatory guidance document on unmanned aircraft systems licensing and operations
notes: Primary regulatory anchor. Applicability and relationship to CAP 722A, 722B, 722H not specified in requirements.

definition: CAA Publication 722A - supplementary guidance to CAP 722 covering specific aspects of UAS operations
notes: Assumed to be extension of CAP 722. Content and scope not detailed.

definition: CAA Publication 722B - supplementary guidance to CAP 722 covering additional UAS regulatory topics
notes: Assumed to be extension of CAP 722. Content and scope not detailed.

definition: CAA Publication 722H - supplementary guidance to CAP 722 specifically addressing security classification handling and MOD-related evidence in UAS authorizations
notes: Referenced in context of classified information. Scope and requirements not summarized.

definition: CAA Publication 1616 - guidance on stakeholder and public engagement for aviation activities
notes: Referenced as basis for REQ017 (public/stakeholder engagement). Applicability to UAS trials undefined.

definition: Joint Authorities for Rulemaking on Unmanned Systems Specific Operational Risk Assessment version 2.5 - international consensus framework for assessing UAS operational risks
notes: Version 2.5 explicitly cited. Relationship to EU regulatory harmonization and UK post-Brexit adoption unclear.

definition: UK Regulatory Approval document series addressing UAS operations; specific topics and content not elaborated
notes: Acronym not expanded. Whether RA stands for Regulatory Approval or another term undefined. Coverage of each series unknown.

definition: UK Defence Standard for safety and airworthiness in unmanned aircraft systems, specifically Part 9 addressing autonomy and decision-making
notes: Military standard. Applicability to civilian CAA authorization unclear. Scope of Part 9 undefined.

definition: UK Joint Service Publication 936 Part 1 - defence policy or procedure document on unmanned aircraft operations
notes: Military publication. Scope and relationship to CAA requirements unknown.

definition: UK Joint Service Publication 440 - guidance on handling classified information in defence operations and MOD-led activities
notes: Referenced in context of classified MOD evidence. Security classification procedures and CAA interface not detailed.

definition: General Data Protection Regulation - EU data protection law affecting processing of personal data, including by UK organizations
notes: Acronym not expanded. Scope relative to UK post-Brexit data protection law (UK GDPR / Data Protection Act 2018) unclear.

definition: Containing all required elements such that no additional information or documentation is necessary to satisfy the requirement
notes: Used in 'complete Operational Authorisation pack' and 'complete recorded evidence'. Criteria for completeness not specified in either context. Ambiguous term.

definition: ASTM Standard AOP-15 addressing operational procedures or performance for unmanned aircraft systems
notes: Standard referenced. Scope relative to DO-365 and F3442 unclear.

definition: Article 36 of the Regulation (EU) 2018/1139 (or UK equivalent) covering specific operational authorizations for unmanned aircraft
notes: Legal reference. Applicability post-Brexit and relationship to CAA processes unclear.

definition: Additional Protocol I to the 1949 Geneva Conventions on international humanitarian law; role in UAS operations context unclear
notes: Formal name 'AP I Geneva' suggests military context. Why referenced in civilian UAS trial requirements unclear.

definition: American Society for Testing and Materials standard F3442 on Detect-and-Avoid system performance
notes: Full form: ASTM F3442. Acronym ASTM not expanded in requirements.

definition: European Organization for Civil Aviation Equipment standard ED-267 on unmanned aircraft systems or related systems
notes: Acronym not expanded. Scope relative to other standards undefined.

definition: European Organization for Civil Aviation Equipment - standards body for aviation equipment and systems
notes: Acronym expanded in context of ED-267.

definition: RTCA DO-365 standard for Detect-and-Avoid performance and certification
notes: RTCA acronym not expanded in requirements. Full form: RTCA Document 365.

definition: Radio Technical Commission for Aeronautics - US standards body for aviation systems
notes: Acronym not expanded in user stories but standard in aerospace.
